title: coffea coursiana subsp. coursiana
intro: Coffea coursiana subsp. coursiana is a lesser-known species of the Coffea genus, which belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is native to Madagascar. It has been found to have medicinal properties due to its rich content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Coffea coursiana subsp. coursiana include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever and infections. Herbal preparations of Coffea coursiana subsp. coursiana can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
